ORDER GRANTING MOTION OF DEFENDANT MCKENZIE, ROSE & HOLLIDAY, INC., TO COMPEL ADDITIONAL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: SUNG E. SHIM, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, MCKENZIE, ROSE & HOLLIDAY DEVELOPMENT, INC. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ADDITIONAL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. THE OBJECTIONS TO THE INTERROGATORIES ARE OVERRULED. RESPONSES THAT ASSERT THAT INVESTIGATION AND DISCOVERY ARE CONTINUING DO NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENT THAT A PARTY RESPOND FULLY AT THE TIME THE RESPONSE IS MADE. THE AMENDMENT OF RESPONSES ARISES OUT OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND NOT OUT OF A RESERVATION OF A RIGHT TO AMEND SET FORTH IN A RESPONSE. THE ARGUMENT THAT RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES MAY BE FOUND IN FORM INTERROGATORIES OR REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS HAS NO MERIT. A PARTY IS NOT LIMITED TO ASKING A RESPONSE TO A PARTICULAR AREA OF INQUIRY THROUGH ONLY ONE TYPE OF DISCOVERY DEVICE. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
